title: Catechism for Anti-Abolitionists (Pro-Choice and Pro-Life)
author: James Silberman
orientation: A satirical catechism, adapted from William Lloyd Garrison's 'A Short Catechism,' that exposes how both pro-choice and pro-life justifications for tolerating abortion collapse into a single dehumanizing premise: that the victims are preborn. Each rhetorical question is answered with the same indictment to highlight the moral inconsistency of refusing to abolish abortion immediately.

questions.question: Why is abortion not considered sinful by those who defend it?
questions.answer: According to the catechism's biting refrain, the only reason abortion escapes the label of sin is that its victims are preborn and thus dehumanized in the eyes of those who tolerate it. The killing itself is identical to murder; only the age and visibility of the victim shield it from moral condemnation.
questions.quote:
1) Why is abortion not sinful?  
Because the victims are preborn.

questions.question: Why do self-proclaimed champions of equality and justice support abortion?
questions.answer: Their entire framework of equality has a hidden exclusion clause: it does not extend to the preborn. The catechism exposes this contradiction by giving the same one-sentence answer that names the excluded class.
questions.quote:
2) Why do those who most fancy themselves the champions of equality and justice support abortion?  
Because their victims are preborn.

questions.question: Why is it considered wrong or judgmental to call abortion 'murder'?
questions.answer: Calling abortion 'murder' is treated as impolite only because society has agreed to discount the personhood of the preborn. Were the victims visible and post-natal, the same act would be universally named what it is.
questions.quote:
3) Why is it wrong and judgemental to call abortion “murder?”  
Because the victims are preborn.

questions.question: Why is it claimed to be unconstitutional to abolish abortion immediately?
questions.answer: The supposed constitutional barrier exists only because the preborn have been categorically denied the constitutional protections owed to every human being. Strip away that one exception and the immediate abolition becomes obviously lawful.
questions.quote:
4) Why is it unconstitutional to abolish abortion immediately?  
Because those who have been denied all constitutional rights are preborn.

questions.question: Why must poverty, mental illness, and every social problem be solved before abolition can even be discussed?
questions.answer: This endless prerequisite list is only deployed in the case of abortion because its victims are preborn. No one demands that other categories of murder wait until society is perfected before being criminalized.
questions.quote:
5) Why must poverty, mental illness, and every social malady be cured before the subject of abolition be raised?  
Because the victims of abortion are preborn.

questions.question: Why must we pursue gradual rather than immediate strategies to end abortion?
questions.answer: Gradualism is tolerated for abortion uniquely because the victims are preborn. We would never accept a multi-decade phase-out for the murder of any other class of human beings.
questions.quote:
6) Why must we pursue gradual strategies to abolish abortion?  
Because those children in need of saving are preborn.

questions.question: Why is immediate abolition derided as wrong, imprudent, and impracticable?
questions.answer: These objections are reserved for the preborn alone. Immediate abolition is considered the only acceptable response to every other form of murder; only the preborn must wait.
questions.quote:
7) Why is immediate abolition wrong, imprudent, and impracticable?  
Because the victims of abortion are preborn.

questions.question: Why is regulating when, where, and how someone can be murdered treated as a just and pragmatic strategy?
questions.answer: Such regulatory schemes are accepted as 'pro-life wisdom' only because the victims are preborn. Applied to any other class of victim, the same approach would be recognized as monstrous.
questions.quote:
8) Why is dictating when, where, and how someone can be murdered a just and pragmatic strategy?  
Because the victims of abortion are preborn.

questions.question: Why must we submit to the Supreme Court's opinion that abortion is legal?
questions.answer: Judicial supremacy is invoked here because the court's targets are preborn. The catechism implies that no honest Christian or constitutionalist would defer to a court ruling marking any other class of human being for slaughter.
questions.quote:
9) Why must we bow to the supreme court’s obviously false, obviously evil opinion that abortion is legal?  
Because those the court has marked for slaughter are preborn.

questions.question: Why is criminalizing abortion called cruel?
questions.answer: The 'cruelty' charge gains traction only because it dismisses the preborn as the relevant party. Real cruelty is the killing itself; calling its prohibition cruel is only possible if the victim does not count.
questions.quote:
10) Why is it cruel to criminalize abortion?  
Because the victims are preborn.

questions.question: Why are abolitionists dismissed as extremists, cultists, and monomaniacs?
questions.answer: The label sticks only because the people they plead for are preborn. Pleading singlemindedly for any visible class of murder victims would be lauded, not mocked.
questions.quote:
11) Why are abolitionists extremists, cultists, and monomaniacs?  
Because those for whom they plead are preborn.

questions.question: Why are pulpits silent on abolition?
questions.answer: Pastoral silence is excused because the parties needing pulpit advocacy are preborn. A faithful pulpit would not justify silence about the murder of any other neighbor.
questions.quote:
12) Why are the pulpits right to be silent on abolition?  
Because those whom preachers would have to speak up for are preborn.

questions.question: Why do pro-life politicians invoke nullification when convenient yet call it a 'debunked legal theory' for abortion?
questions.answer: The double standard exists because the people who would benefit from interposition are preborn. The same politicians readily nullify federal action on issues whose victims are visible and politically prized.
questions.quote:
13) Why is nullification often employed by the same pro-life politicians who call nullification a “debunked legal theory” when it comes to abortion?  
Because those who would be interposed for are preborn.

questions.question: Why is it sufficient merely to defund abortion rather than abolish it?
questions.answer: Settling for defunding instead of criminalization is only thinkable because the surviving victims are preborn. No one would ever say the murder of toddlers is adequately addressed by withdrawing federal funding.
questions.quote:
14) Why is it sufficient to call for the defunding of abortion rather than the abolition of abortion?  
Because the victims of abortion are preborn.

questions.question: Why is 'empowering women to choose life' prioritized above abolishing abortion?
questions.answer: This priority inversion only makes sense if the victims do not count fully. Because the preborn are dehumanized, persuasion campaigns aimed at the killer are treated as more urgent than legal protection of the killed.
questions.quote:
15) Why is “empowering women to choose life” more important than the abolition of abortion?  
Because the victims of abortion are preborn.

questions.question: Why are abolitionists said to be wrong in both principles and methods?
questions.answer: The blanket condemnation of abolitionist principles and tactics is sustained only by the unspoken premise that the preborn matter less. Apply abolitionist principles to any other class of victim and they are uncontroversial.
questions.quote:
16) Why are abolitionists wrong in their principles and measures?  
Because the victims of abortion are preborn.

questions.question: Why should the priests and Levites of our society who pass by on the other side not be sternly rebuked?
questions.answer: Echoing the parable of the Good Samaritan, the catechism notes that society excuses the modern priests and Levites only because the wounded neighbor is preborn. Faithful rebuke is owed precisely because every neighbor, born or unborn, deserves the response of a Samaritan, not a passerby.
questions.quote:
17) Why ought not the priests and levites of our society passing by on the other side be sternly rebuked?  
Because those being led to slaughter are preborn.

questions.question: What is the source and structure of this catechism?
questions.answer: Silberman explicitly modeled the piece on William Lloyd Garrison's nineteenth-century abolitionist work, drawing a direct line between the rhetorical strategies used to excuse chattel slavery and those used today to excuse abortion. The shared single-line answer mirrors Garrison's style of exposing systemic dehumanization.
questions.quote: The above catechism is adapted from William Lloyd Garrison’s *A Short Catechism*.

related.title: Immediate, Not Gradual Abolition of Abortion
related.reason: Develops the core thesis behind catechism questions 6 and 7: gradualism is morally indefensible and only immediate abolition is consistent with the personhood of the preborn.

related.title: Incrementalism Versus Immediatism in a Nutshell
related.reason: A concise companion to the catechism's repeated jab at incremental and gradual strategies, framing the same debate the catechism satirizes.

related.title: Five Reasons Abolitionists Oppose Incremental Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Provides the substantive arguments behind the catechism's mockery of regulatory and incremental schemes (questions 8 and 14).

related.title: Are Nullification's Advocates the “Villains” of American History?
related.reason: Directly addresses the nullification double standard called out in question 13 of the catechism.

related.title: Contra Klusendorf and Desiring God: Pro-Life Compromise Dooms Preborn Children
related.reason: Expands the catechism's accusation that pro-life compromise (questions 5–8, 14–15) abandons the preborn it claims to defend.

related.title: The Holiness of God Destroys Pro-Life Incrementalism
related.reason: Theological backbone for the catechism's implicit charge against silent pulpits (question 12) and gradualist Christianity.

related.title: Refuting Toby Sumpter's “Eight Tenets of Smashmouth Incrementalism”
related.reason: Another point-by-point rebuttal of the incrementalist logic the catechism lampoons line by line.
